differential_diagnoses: Legionella pneumophila pneumonia with severe rhabdomyolysis and acute kidney injury
Invasive aspergillosis (possibly secondary to underlying immunocompromise or immune dysregulation)
Antisynthetase syndrome (interstitial lung disease and severe myositis)
Severe influenza pneumonia with secondary rhabdomyolysis
Acute exacerbation of occult underlying genetic myopathy (e.g., nemaline myopathy variant)
next_steps: Legionella urinary antigen testing and respiratory culture
Urgent hemodialysis for management of severe hyperkalemia, metabolic acidosis, and renal failure
Bronchoscopy with bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) for microbiological and cytology assessment
Muscle biopsy for histopathologic evaluation of severe rhabdomyolysis
Serologic evaluation for myositis-specific antibodies (e.g., anti-Jo-1, anti-SRP, anti-HMGCR)
Serial monitoring of creatinine kinase and renal function
Broadening antimicrobial coverage (e.g., levofloxacin for Legionella, consider antifungal therapy)

differential_diagnoses: Legionella pneumophila pneumonia with rhabdomyolysis and acute kidney injury
Inflammatory myopathy (e.g., antisynthetase syndrome triggering acute respiratory/renal failure)
Severe influenza or viral pneumonia complicated by secondary bacterial superinfection and rhabdomyolysis
next_steps: Urgent hemodialysis for management of severe acute kidney injury, hyperkalemia, and metabolic acidosis
Legionella urinary antigen testing and respiratory culture
Broad-spectrum antimicrobial therapy including coverage for Legionella (e.g., levofloxacin)
Blood and urine cultures for bacterial pathogens
Bronchoscopy with bronchoalveolar lavage for culture, cytology, and fungal staining
Urinalysis (to assess for myoglobinuria and cast formation)
Serial monitoring of creatine kinase, electrolytes, and renal function
